name: postgresql-optimization
description: PostgreSQL database optimization workflow for query tuning, indexing strategies, performance analysis, and production database management.
capabilities: Analyze PostgreSQL query performance using EXPLAIN ANALYZE
Design and implement indexing strategies including B-tree, composite, and partial indexes
Tune PostgreSQL configuration parameters like shared_buffers and work_mem
Schedule and manage VACUUM and ANALYZE maintenance operations
Set up monitoring and alerting for database performance metrics
Identify and resolve database bottlenecks through systematic assessment
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 36 potential issues (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithm) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill file contains only markdown documentation with skill references and prompts. No actual code execution, cryptographic operations, or security risks exist. The detected patterns are markdown code blocks and skill name references misinterpreted by the scanner.
valueStatement: PostgreSQL databases can suffer from slow queries and poor performance without proper optimization. This skill provides a systematic workflow for query tuning, indexing, and configuration management.
limitations: Requires existing database access and appropriate permissions
Cannot modify application code that generates queries
Does not include hardware provisioning or infrastructure changes
Skill relies on other referenced skills for full functionality

useCases.title: Resolve Slow Query Performance
useCases.description: Systematically analyze and optimize slow-running queries in production PostgreSQL databases using EXPLAIN ANALYZE and indexing strategies.

useCases.title: Design Indexing Strategy
useCases.description: Create comprehensive indexing plans including B-tree, composite, and partial indexes to improve query performance across multiple tables.

useCases.title: Tune PostgreSQL Configuration
useCases.description: Optimize PostgreSQL server settings like shared_buffers, work_mem, and effective_cache_size for better resource utilization.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Performance Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @database-optimizer to assess PostgreSQL performance on my database. Check version, review configuration, and identify slow queries.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new PostgreSQL optimization project

promptTemplates.title: Query Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt:
Use @sql-optimization-patterns to analyze and optimize the following queries. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE and suggest improvements:

```sql
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at > '2024-01-01';
```

promptTemplates.title: Indexing Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @database-design to design PostgreSQL indexing strategy for our e-commerce database with tables for users, orders, products, and reviews.
promptTemplates.scenario: Designing indexes for a new application

promptTemplates.title: Configuration Tuning
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @postgres-best-practices to tune PostgreSQL configuration for a database server with 8GB RAM serving 500 concurrent users.

bestPractices: Always run EXPLAIN ANALYZE before and after query optimizations to measure actual improvements
Create indexes during low-traffic periods as index creation locks tables
Monitor query performance over time using pg_stat_statements to identify regression
antiPatterns: Creating too many indexes - each index adds write overhead and consumes storage
Using sequential scans when indexes are available due to misconfiguration
Ignoring table bloat and vacuum settings - leads to performance degradation over time

faq.question: What PostgreSQL versions are supported?
faq.answer: This workflow supports PostgreSQL 9.6 and later versions, including PostgreSQL 14, 15, and 16.

faq.question: Does this skill require database admin privileges?
faq.answer: Yes, some operations require superuser or owner privileges for configuration changes and maintenance tasks.

faq.question: Can this skill optimize queries in any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill optimizes the SQL queries themselves regardless of the application language (Python, Node.js, Ruby, etc.).

faq.question: How long does a typical optimization project take?
faq.answer: A basic assessment takes 1-2 hours. Full optimization including indexing and tuning may take 1-3 days depending on database size.

faq.question: Will creating indexes cause downtime?
faq.answer: CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY can lock writes. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for production databases to avoid downtime.

faq.question: How do I monitor improvements after optimization?
faq.answer: Use pg_stat_statements to track query times, set up pgBadger for log analysis, and create Grafana dashboards for ongoing monitoring.
